rationale_for_baseline_tier: This presentation needs an in-person genital examination, a point-of-care blood glucose and ketones, and urgent bloods that the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely — the centre of gravity of an examination- and lab-gated urological/metabolic case. The patient is currently haemodynamically stable, afebrile, not vomiting, with no necrosis, no paraphimosis and no retention, so this is not an immediate life threat; but newly discovered marked hyperglycaemia with systemic osmotic symptoms plus an un-examinable recurrent genital infection floors the correct action at urgent same-day to next-day in-person assessment (T3), not routine care. Under-triage below T3 risks missing evolving diabetic ketoacidosis and worsening phimosis; over-triage to an emergency tier without any red-flag feature is the over-reaction to resist.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Diabetic ketoacidosis features — vomiting, drowsiness or confusion, abdominal pain, rapid deep breathing, or inability to keep fluids down
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 / go to ED now
escalation_edges.rationale: Uncorrected marked hyperglycaemia can progress to diabetic ketoacidosis, a metabolic emergency requiring IV fluids, insulin and monitoring.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you start vomiting, become drowsy or confused, get belly pain, breathe fast and deep, or can't keep fluids down, call 000 or go to the emergency department straight away — this can be a dangerous diabetic emergency.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Redness spreading rapidly to the scrotum or lower abdomen, skin turning black or necrotic, severe pain out of proportion, or high fever with feeling very unwell
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 / go to ED now
escalation_edges.rationale: These are the warning signs of Fournier's gangrene / necrotising fasciitis, a surgical emergency with high mortality if delayed.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads quickly to your scrotum or belly, the skin turns black, the pain becomes severe, or you get a high fever and feel very unwell, call 000 or go to the emergency department immediately.

escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same hour — go to ED / urgent care immediately
escalation_edges.rationale: Acute urinary retention from preputial swelling/phimosis needs urgent catheterisation, which the AI cannot provide.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you become completely unable to pass urine, go to an emergency department or urgent care straight away — you may need a catheter.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Foreskin becomes trapped behind the head of the penis with a swollen, dusky or increasingly painful glans (paraphimosis)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 / go to ED now; do not force the foreskin
escalation_edges.rationale: Paraphimosis strangulates the glans and is a tissue-necrosis emergency; forcing retraction can cause it.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Never force the foreskin back. If it does get stuck behind the head of the penis and the head becomes swollen, dusky or very painful, go to the emergency department or call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Newly suspected, uncontrolled diabetes with marked hyperglycaemia — immunocompromised host
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold to escalate: ensure same-day blood-glucose testing, warn explicitly about DKA and necrotising infection, and treat any systemic deterioration as an emergency rather than a routine flare.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Has not accessed medical care in years and attends alone
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give a concrete, named pathway (specific after-hours GP / urgent care) and confirm the patient can and will attend; reinforce the emergency red flags clearly given there is no one else present.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Arrange an urgent in-person appointment today or tomorrow — an after-hours GP or urgent care clinic — for a genital examination and an urgent blood-sugar test, because the constant thirst, heavy urination and exhaustion suggest your blood sugar may be very high and needs checking urgently. In the meantime wash gently with lukewarm water only, stop all sugary drinks and drink plain water, and never force the foreskin back. Go straight to an emergency department or call 000 if the redness spreads, the skin turns black, you develop a fever with vomiting, you become drowsy or short of breath, the head of the penis gets trapped and dusky, or you cannot pass urine.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent in-person GP / after-hours GP / urgent care today; 000 or ED for red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: An after-hours GP or urgent care clinic can see you tonight for the examination and a blood-sugar check; go to ED if any red-flag warning sign appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Bring the six-month history of recurrent genital infection and the recent thirst, heavy urination and fatigue; the reviewing clinician will need to examine the genitalia and arrange fasting glucose/HbA1c, urea/electrolytes/creatinine, urine microscopy and culture, and commence diabetes management.
